mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year historical backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative forward returns in greater than 50% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates accelerating manufacturing output and economic momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns with a statistical significance level below 0.10 across multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive federal funds rates create stagflationary pressure that compresses corporate margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change exceeds 3.0% and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive forward returns in greater than 55% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day returns across multiple macro cycles with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent quarter-over-quarter confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above the threshold fails to produce positive forward returns in greater than 45% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate signal synchronized economic strengthening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The synchronized signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 50% of triggered instances over a 10-year sample.
